name: SMH-Bench
description: SMH-Bench is a benchmark for evaluating LLM-driven agents in simulated smart-home environments. It contains 1,100 human-audited, environment-grounded task instances across seven capability categories and 22 fine-grained subcategories, stratified by simple, medium, and complex home configurations.
citation: Anonymous Authors. SMH-Bench: Towards Realistic Evaluation of LLM-Driven Agents in Smart-Home Environments. NeurIPS 2026 submission.

recordSet.name: SMH-Bench task instances
recordSet.description: One JSON object per benchmark instance. Each instance contains a user instruction, optional dialogue history and memory, an executable home engine snapshot or fallback engine, ground-truth labels, and task-specific evaluation criteria.

field.name: uuid
field.description: Stable unique identifier for the benchmark instance.

field.name: query_idx
field.description: Index of the query within its source subset.

field.name: history
field.description: Reference dialogue trajectory for multi-turn or clarification tasks.

field.name: initial_chat_history
field.description: Prior dialogue context needed by context-aware tasks when present.

field.name: memory_list
field.description: User-memory entries used by memory-grounded tasks when present.

field.name: memory_hit
field.description: Memory entries expected to be relevant when present.

field.name: entrance
field.description: Optional entry device identifier for tool-based evaluation.

field.name: engine
field.description: Executable serialized HomeEnv state when stored inline.

field.name: ground_truth
field.description: Target labels, automation conditions, or expected query response.

field.name: critic
field.description: Boolean evaluator predicate over final home state when applicable.

field.name: intended_query
field.description: Resolved intended query for selected ambiguous cases when present.

recordSet.name: HomeEnv engine snapshot
recordSet.description: Serialized smart-home state used as the shared fallback engine for complex tasks. It contains room topology, devices, attributes, services, and current values.

field.name: home
field.description: Root home object containing rooms and devices.

rai:dataLimitations: The benchmark uses simulated smart-home environments rather than physical homes, so it does not capture real sensor noise, network latency, vendor-specific device behavior, device failures, or safety-critical recovery policies.

The tasks focus on language-driven decision making over structured home states. The dataset does not evaluate multimodal perception, physical manipulation, continuous adaptation to real users, or long-horizon household learning.
The benchmark is intended for evaluation and diagnosis of smart-home agents, not for direct deployment decisions in safety-critical or privacy-sensitive homes.
rai:dataBiases: Task instructions and reference trajectories were generated with LLM assistance and then validated and audited, so the language distribution may reflect the prompting templates, generator preferences, and Chinese-centric household naming patterns.

Device types, room layouts, and routines are designed to stress benchmark capabilities and may not represent all geographic regions, cultures, accessibility needs, household sizes, or smart-home ecosystems.
The category distribution intentionally allocates more instances to complex reasoning tasks, so aggregate scores should be interpreted together with category-level and home-complexity-level results.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The released benchmark does not contain real household logs, real user identities, addresses, demographic attributes, health records, or private sensor traces.
Memory entries and dialogue histories are synthetic artifacts created for evaluation of preference and context reasoning. They should not be interpreted as real user data.
rai:dataUseCases: Recommended uses include evaluating LLM-driven smart-home agents, comparing direct reasoning and tool-interactive protocols, diagnosing state grounding failures, testing automation-rule construction, and studying ambiguity handling, multi-turn context tracking, personalized memory use, and environment-grounded querying.

The dataset is not recommended for training deployable home-control systems without additional real-world validation, safety review, user-consent mechanisms, access control, and confirmation policies for high-impact actions.
rai:dataSocialImpact: SMH-Bench may improve smart-home agent reliability by exposing failures before deployment, especially failures in state grounding, clarification policy, automation synthesis, and unintended device changes. Misuse risks include overclaiming readiness for real-home deployment or optimizing agents for simulated tasks while ignoring privacy, safety, security, and accessibility requirements in real households. The release mitigates these risks by using synthetic environments and by documenting intended and non-intended uses.

prov:wasDerivedFrom.name: Synthetic HomeEnv smart-home state space and task templates

prov:wasGeneratedBy: Home instance construction: simple and medium tasks are paired with independently generated smart-home environments; complex tasks use a shared large-scale home with 31 nested rooms and 135 devices.

Task and instruction generation: GPT-5-assisted prompts generated structured task records and natural-language user instructions from serialized home states, target categories, and task-specific constraints.

Simulator validation: HomeEnv checked device existence, operation support, argument validity, automation triggers, query answers, and replay consistency between labels, critics, and executable home states.
Human audit: samples were manually reviewed for instruction naturalness, category alignment, ground-truth consistency, clarification appropriateness, and absence of context or memory conflicts.
